title: 3 Monster Dividend Stocks to Hold for the Next 10 Years
author: Keith Speights
description: The article recommends three dividend stocks for long-term investors seeking stable income and growth: Enterprise Products Partners (EPD), a midstream energy company with 27 years of consecutive distribution increases and a 5.6% yield; Evergy (EVRG), a utility stock benefiting from AI data center expansion in Kansas and Missouri with an 8%+ annual EPS growth forecast and 3.4% dividend yield; and United Parcel Service (UPS), a logistics leader with a 6.8% dividend yield positioned for profitability improvements as it restructures its business and reduces Amazon dependence.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong midstream energy company with 27 consecutive years of distribution increases, 5.6% yield, inflation protection through 90% of contracts with price escalation clauses, stable fee-based revenue model, and long-term growth drivers from LNG exports and AI data center demand.

Utility stock with monopoly in service areas, 50% clean energy generation, significant AI data center growth opportunities in Kansas and Missouri region, expected 8%+ annual EPS growth from 2028 onwards, and 23 consecutive years of dividend increases with 3.4% yield.

Global logistics leader positioned for turnaround with 2026 as inflection point for restructuring strategy, expected profitability improvements from higher-margin shipments like healthcare logistics, strong 6.8% dividend yield with sufficient free cash flow to support it, and bright long-term prospects despite recent stock volatility.
Mentioned contextually as UPS is reducing Amazon shipment volume as part of its restructuring strategy; no direct investment recommendation or sentiment expressed about Amazon itself.

title: Oil Glut, Wind Freeze, and Energy Policy in the Year Ahead
author: Motley Fool Staff
description: Energy investors face mixed signals in 2026 as oil prices remain depressed due to global oversupply, while renewable energy projects face policy headwinds from the Trump administration's pause on offshore wind projects. Despite challenges, analysts highlight opportunities in well-capitalized midstream companies, cost-efficient oil producers, and renewable energy leaders positioned to benefit from long-term demand trends and infrastructure spending.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong operator with good cost controls, but stock is a value trap due to global supply fundamentals and OPEC's 'lower for longer' narrative. Trades at 11x earnings but faces commodity price volatility.
Well-run independent oil producer with earnings resilience, but exposed to commodity price volatility. Can operate profitably at $50 oil but faces uncertain long-term pricing environment.
Large integrated oil company suitable as dividend player, but lacks competitive moats. Performance tied to commodity prices and geopolitical factors.
Only major foreign oil company operating in Venezuela; vulnerable to escalating geopolitical conflict in the region, which could negatively impact operations and stock performance.
Midstream pipeline operator positioned to benefit from potential Venezuela conflict escalation, data center construction surge, and coal-to-natural gas power plant transitions.

North American midstream leader positioned to benefit from increased oil exports, data center infrastructure buildout, and potential permitting reform legislation.
Refiner with strong cost advantages, petrochemical manufacturing, and midstream assets. Well-run dividend growth investment with resilience to long-term commodity headwinds.
Residential solar company with cost advantages and ecosystem moat. Benefits from declining interest rates, rising utility costs, and continued cash generation despite policy headwinds.
Residential solar inverter company with ecosystem advantages. Positioned to benefit from declining interest rates and rising energy costs despite near-term policy uncertainty.
Well-capitalized utility-scale renewable developer with ability to acquire assets during downturns. Positioned to benefit from lower renewable costs and long-term energy demand growth.

title: Worried About Trump's New Tariffs? These 3 U.S. Companies Are Set to Weather the Storm
author: The Motley Fool
description: Despite concerns over Trump's tariffs, three U.S. companies - Kroger, Evergy, and Vertex Pharmaceuticals - are well-positioned to weather the storm. Kroger's domestic focus and private-label products, Evergy's essential utility services, and Vertex's innovative drug pipeline make them resilient to trade policy changes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Kroger is well-positioned to weather the tariff storm due to its domestic focus and ability to benefit from increased sales of its private-label products.
Evergy, a utility company serving Kansas and Missouri, is not significantly affected by tariffs as its customers will continue to need its essential electricity services.

Vertex Pharmaceuticals is tariff-resistant due to its innovative drug pipeline, including therapies for cystic fibrosis, rare blood diseases, and pain management, which are not impacted by trade policies.

title: FCC Approves Notice of Proposed Rulemaking to Expand 900 MHz Broadband Segment
author: N/A
description: The FCC has proposed expanding the 900 MHz broadband spectrum from 3 MHz by 3 MHz to 5 MHz by 5 MHz, which would support growing demand for private, secure wireless broadband networks for utilities, critical infrastructure, and enterprise entities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Anterix is mentioned as a utility technology company that expressed gratitude to the FCC for the proposed rule changes, which would support the growth of the 900 MHz broadband segment that Anterix is focused on.
Ameren is quoted as stating that adopting a voluntary process to create a 5/5 MHz broadband option is the right thing for the Commission to do to help utilities prepare for the future.

Evergy is quoted as stating that access to broadband capabilities, especially the proposed expanded 5/5 MHz option in the 900 MHz band, will play a vital role in enabling utilities to design, install, and operate technically advanced networks.

Xcel Energy is quoted as stating that access to expanded 5/5 MHz broadband networks will provide utilities with additional capacity to support 900 MHz pLTE broadband deployment with enhanced broadband communications capabilities.

title: 3 Utility Stocks That Will Benefit from Less Regulation
author: Marketbeat.Com
description: The article discusses three utility stocks that are expected to benefit from a more business-friendly regulatory environment in the coming years, with a focus on their earnings growth, dividend yields, and capital investment plans.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Evergy's diverse energy generation mix, its backlog of projects including data centers and an EV battery manufacturing facility, and its consistent dividend growth, indicating a positive outlook for the company.

Duke Energy is expected to benefit from population migration into its service areas and regulatory settlements that provide predictability for its revenue and earnings, as well as its high-yield dividend that has been increasing for 20 consecutive years.
